name: Bahraini Balaleet (Sweet Saffron Rice with Egg)
steps: Boil the noodles until tender, then toss with the sugar and cardamom.
Scramble the eggs separately and serve alongside.

ingredients_full.note: or thin wheat vermicelli, crushed into short pieces
pinch, steeped in 1 tbsp hot water

steps_detailed: Boil the vermicelli in water 3-4 minutes until just tender, then drain well.
Melt the butter in a pan, stir in the sugar, cardamom, saffron with its soaking water, and rosewater, then toss in the drained vermicelli until coated and slightly caramelized, 3-4 minutes.
Push the sweet vermicelli to one side of the pan; crack the eggs into the other side and scramble gently until just set, keeping the two separate.

Serve the sweet vermicelli and the plain egg side by side, scattered with the pistachios - the sweetness of the noodles against the plain savory egg is the whole point of balaleet; they're eaten together in the same bite, not mixed into one dish.

name: Bahraini Khameer Bread with Honey
steps: Mix the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and enough warm water to form a soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Pat into a round and cook on a hot, lightly oiled griddle until puffed and golden on both sides.
Split and drizzle with the honey and melted ghee.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, cardamom, a pinch of salt, and enough warm water to form a soft, slightly tacky dough. Cover and rest 25-30 minutes.
Pat into a round about half an inch thick and cook on a hot, lightly oiled griddle, 3-4 minutes per side, until deeply golden and cooked through.
Split while warm and drizzle generously with the honey and melted ghee - khameer is a near-daily staple across Bahraini bakeries.

name: Bahraini Chickpea and Tomato Breakfast

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Warm the chickpeas and tomatoes with the cumin in the oil, mashing lightly.
Serve with warm bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Warm the chickpeas and tomatoes with the cumin and garlic in the oil over medium heat, 6-7 minutes, mashing lightly.
Finish with the lime juice and serve with warm bread - a simple, filling breakfast common in Bahraini homes before the day's work begins.

name: Bahraini Fish Machboos
steps: Simmer the rice with the tomatoes and turmeric in 1.5 cups water, cover, and cook 20-22 minutes.
Pan-fry the fish separately, 3-4 minutes per side, and serve over the rice.
steps_detailed: Season the fish with a little of the cinnamon, cardamom, and pepper, and pan-fry in a little of the oil until just cooked through and lightly browned, 3-4 minutes per side; set aside.
In the same pot, soften the onion and garlic in the remaining oil over medium heat, 6-8 minutes, until deeply golden.
Add the tomatoes, remaining cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, and pepper, and cook 3-4 minutes until fragrant.
Add 1.5 cups water, the pierced dried limes, and the saffron, and bring to a simmer.

Stir in the rice, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed - the whole dried limes stay in the pot, releasing their sour, slightly bitter perfume through the rice as it cooks; they're not eaten, just discarded or pushed aside at the table.
Fluff the rice, top with the fish, and scatter with the cilantro.

Machboos, rice slow-cooked in a spiced stock under whole dried limes, is the Gulf's defining dish - the loomi is what makes it recognizably machboos rather than a generic spiced pilaf; without it the dish loses its most characteristic flavor.

name: Bahraini Chicken Machboos
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the rice, tomatoes, turmeric, and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes until the rice is tender.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Brown the chicken in the oil over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes, then set aside.
Soften the onion and garlic in the same pot, 6-8 minutes, until deeply golden.
Add the tomatoes, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, and pepper, and cook 3-4 minutes until fragrant.
Return the chicken to the pot with 1.75 cups water, the pierced dried limes, and the saffron, and simmer 20-25 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Lift out the chicken, stir the rice into the remaining liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes until tender.
Fluff the rice, top with the chicken and raisins.
This is the most common everyday version of machboos - chicken, dried lime, and whole warm spices, built the same way whether it's a Tuesday dinner or a wedding feast.

name: Bahraini Harees
steps: Simmer the chicken with the onion until very tender, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Add the bulgur to the broth and simmer, mashing often, until it forms a thick, smooth porridge.
Stir the chicken back in, top with the ghee, and serve.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion in 2.5 cups water for 25-30 minutes until very tender, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Add the bulgur to the broth and simmer 25-30 minutes, mashing frequently, until it forms a thick, smooth porridge.
Stir most of the shredded chicken back in, top with the ghee and a dusting of cinnamon, and serve - harees is prepared in enormous communal pots in Bahrain during Ramadan and Ashura.

name: Bahraini Thareed
steps: Simmer the chicken with the potato and tomatoes until tender, about 25-30 minutes.
Tear the flatbread into a bowl and ladle the chicken and broth over it, letting it soak.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the potato, tomatoes, onion, and turmeric in a splash of water for 28-32 minutes until the chicken is tender.
Tear the flatbread into a wide serving bowl and ladle the chicken, vegetables, and broth generously over it.
Thareed remains a Friday-lunch staple in many Bahraini households, eaten communally from a single large platter.

name: Bahraini Ghuzi (Spiced Lamb over Rice)
steps: Brown the lamb with the onion and cardamom.
Add the rice and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes until the rice is tender.

ingredients_full.note: formed into a few large chunks

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Brown the lamb with the onion in the oil over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes.
Add the cardamom, cinnamon, saffron, and 2 cups water, cover, and simmer 35-40 minutes until very tender.
Lift out the lamb, stir the rice into the remaining liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes until tender.
Toast the almonds in a dry pan, 2-3 minutes, until golden.
Mound the rice on a platter, top with the lamb, and scatter with the toasted almonds and raisins.

Ghuzi, whole spiced lamb served over rice and topped with nuts and raisins, is Bahrain's classic celebration centerpiece - traditionally a whole roasted lamb, this home version keeps the same rice, spicing, and nut-and-raisin topping at a practical scale.

name: Bahraini Lamb Machboos
steps: Brown the lamb with the onion.
Add the rice, tomatoes, turmeric, and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes until the rice is tender.

ingredients_full.note: formed into a few large chunks, or use lamb shoulder pieces if available

steps_detailed: Brown the lamb in the oil over medium-high heat, 9-11 minutes, then set aside.
Soften the onion and garlic in the same pot, 6-8 minutes, until deeply golden.
Add the tomatoes, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, and pepper, and cook 3-4 minutes until fragrant.
Return the lamb to the pot with 2 cups water, the pierced dried limes, and the saffron, cover, and simmer 40-45 minutes until very tender.
Lift out the lamb, stir the rice into the remaining liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes until tender.
Fluff the rice and top with the lamb.
Lamb machboos, richer and slower-cooked than the chicken version, is the centerpiece of Bahraini celebration meals.

name: Bahraini Chicken Saloona
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the potato, tomatoes, and 1 cup water, and simmer 25-30 minutes until the potato is tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion, garlic, cumin, and turmeric over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the potato, tomatoes, and 1.25 cups water, and simmer 28-32 minutes until the potato is fully tender.
Saloona is the reliable weeknight dinner across Bahraini households, easily stretched with whatever vegetables are on hand.

name: Bahraini Fish and Rice Casserole
steps: Layer the cooked rice, fish, tomatoes, and onion in a baking dish.
Bake at 375F for 20-25 minutes until the fish is cooked through.
steps_detailed: Toss the cooked rice with the garlic and turmeric, then layer it with the fish, tomatoes, and onion in a baking dish.

Bake at 375F (190C) for 22-28 minutes until the fish flakes easily and the top is lightly golden - a simple baked layering of fish and rice, popular in Bahraini coastal towns where fresh catch is never far from the table.

name: Bahraini Samboosa
steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Make a simple dough from the flour and water, roll thin, cut into strips, fill, and fold into triangles.
Fry until golden and crisp.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, cumin, and cilantro.
Make a simple, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out thin.
Cut into strips, fill, and fold into tight triangles, sealing the edges well.
Fry until deeply golden and crisp, 4-5 minutes - samboosa are ubiquitous at Bahraini gatherings, especially through Ramadan.

name: Bahraini Khubz with Za'atar and Cheese
steps: Warm the bread on a dry pan.
Scatter with the cheese and za'atar, and drizzle with the oil.
Fold and serve warm.
steps_detailed: Warm the bread on a dry pan over medium heat, 1-2 minutes per side.
Scatter generously with the cheese and za'atar, and drizzle with the oil.
Fold in half and press briefly until the cheese softens.

name: Bahraini Hummus with Cumin and Lime

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and cumin with a splash of water until smooth.
Serve with warm flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, cumin, and garlic with a splash of water until very smooth.
Spoon into a shallow dish and drizzle with the oil.

name: Bahraini Dates Stuffed with Pistachios
steps: Split each date and press the chopped pistachios into the center.
Serve as a simple, sweet snack.
steps_detailed: Split each date lengthwise, dab with a trace of rosewater, and press the chopped pistachios into the center.
Stuffed dates are the classic gesture of Bahraini hospitality, offered to any guest alongside coffee.

name: Bahraini Halwa
steps: Whisk the cornstarch with 3/4 cup water until smooth, then simmer, stirring constantly, until very thick.
Stir in the sugar and saffron, and cook a few more minutes.
Pour into a small dish, scatter with pistachios, and chill until firm.
steps_detailed: Whisk the cornstarch with 3/4 cup water until completely smooth.
Simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 10-12 minutes until very thick and glossy.
Stir in the sugar, saffron, rosewater, and cardamom, and cook 5-6 more minutes, stirring, until it pulls together into a thick, gel-like mass.
Pour into a small lined dish, scatter with pistachios, and chill at least 2-3 hours until fully set.
Bahraini halwa is the country's single most famous culinary export - glossy, deeply aromatic, and traditionally the first thing offered to any visitor.

name: Bahraini Luqaimat

ingredients.note: tbsp, standing in for date syrup

steps: Mix the flour, baking powder, and enough water to form a thick, smooth batter. Rest 20 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry until puffed and golden.
Drizzle with the honey while still warm.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, standing in for date syrup

steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, saffron, and enough warm water to form a thick, smooth batter. Cover and rest 25-30 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry, turning occasionally, 3-5 minutes until puffed and deeply golden.
Drain briefly and drizzle generously with the honey while still warm - a beloved fried sweet at Bahraini iftars.

name: Bahraini Saffron Rice Pudding
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon and honey for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.

ingredients_full.note: pinch, steeped in 1 tsp hot water

steps_detailed: Simmer the basmati in the milk with the saffron and its soaking water, and the cardamom, over low heat, stirring often, 25-28 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Stir in the sugar and rosewater.

Top with the pistachios before serving - basmati's aroma is part of what makes this taste distinctly Gulf rather than like a generic rice pudding; a plainer short-grain rice would cook down softer but lose that perfume entirely.

name: Bahraini Lime and Cilantro Sauce
steps: Combine the garlic, lime juice, oil, and cilantro.
Serve alongside grilled or baked fish.
steps_detailed: Combine the garlic, lime juice, oil, cilantro, and jalapeno.
Let sit 10 minutes so the flavors meld - a bright, simple sauce that pairs especially well with any of Bahrain's many fish dishes.

name: Bahraini Date and Tahini Drizzle

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the dates with the tahini and a splash of water or milk until smooth and pourable.
Drizzle over bread or fresh fruit.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the dates with the tahini, cardamom, and a splash of water or milk until smooth and pourable.
Drizzle over khameer or fresh fruit - the nutty edge from the tahini sets this apart from a plain date syrup.
